title: श्री गायत्री कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गायत्री कवचम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीगायत्रीकवचस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। गायत्री देवता।
सर्वकामनासिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

ॐ गायत्री शीर्षं पातु, सवितृदेवता भालम्।
वेदमाता च नेत्रे मे, पातु विश्वामित्र ऋषिः श्रुती॥ १॥

घ्राणं पातु सदा गायत्री, ओष्ठौ सावित्री सदा।
जिह्वां सरस्वती पातु, कण्ठं पातु च शान्तिदा॥ २॥

बुद्धिं मेधां च हृदयं, त्रिपदा सर्वदा मम।
प्राणं पातु परा शक्तिः, अपानं भुवनेश्वरी॥ ३॥

व्यानं पातु सदा गायत्री, उदानं ब्रह्मचारिणी।
समानं पातु सावित्री, शरीरं सर्वतो मम॥ ४॥

तत् पादं पूर्वतः पातु, सवितुः दक्षिणे सदा।
वरेण्यं पश्चिमे पातु, भर्गो देवस्य उत्तरे॥ ५॥

धीमहि चोर्ध्वतः पातु, धियो यो नः अधस्तथा।
प्रचोदयात् पातु सदा, सर्वतो मां सुरक्षतु॥ ६॥

इतीदं कवचं दिव्यं, गायत्र्या ब्रह्मनिर्मितम्।
यः पठेत् प्रयतो भूत्वा, सर्वान् कामान् अवाप्नुयात्॥ ७॥

विद्यार्थी लभते विद्यां, धनार्थी लभते धनम्।
पुत्रार्थी लभते पुत्रान्, मोक्षार्थी मोक्षम् आप्नुयात्॥ ८॥

सर्वपापहरं दिव्यं, गायत्रीकवचं शुभम्।
त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नित्यं, स याति परमां गतिम्॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री गायत्री कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gayatri Kavacham ||

Om asya Shri Gayatri Kavachasya Brahma Rishih |
Gayatri Chhandah | Gayatri Devata |
Sarva-kamanasiddhyarthe jape viniyogah ||

Om Gayatri shirsham patu, Savitri-devata bhalam |
Vedamata cha netre me, patu Vishwamitra Rishiph shruti || 1 ||

Ghranam patu sada Gayatri, oshthau Savitri sada |
Jihvam Saraswati patu, kantham patu cha shantida || 2 ||

Buddhim medham cha hridayam, tripada sarvada mama |
Pranam patu para shaktih, apanam Bhuvaneshwari || 3 ||

Vyanam patu sada Gayatri, udanam Brahmacharini |
Samanam patu Savitri, shariram sarvato mama || 4 ||

Tat padam purvatah patu, Savituh dakshiney sada |
Varenyam pashchime patu, Bhargo Devasya uttare || 5 ||

Dhimahi chordhvatah patu, dhiyo yo nah adhastatha |
Prachodayat patu sada, sarvato mam surakshat u || 6 ||

Iteedam kavacham divyam, Gayatrya Brahma-nirmitam |
Yah pathet prayato bhutva, sarvan kaman avapnuyat || 7 ||

Vidyarthi labhate vidyam, dhanarthi labhate dhanam |
Putrarthi labhate putran, moksharthi moksham apnuyat || 8 ||

Sarva-papaharam divyam, Gayatri-kavacham shubham |
Trisandhyam yah pathen-nityam, sa yati paramam gatim || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Gayatri Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: इस गायत्री कवच के ऋषि ब्रह्मा हैं, छन्द गायत्री है और देवता गायत्री माता हैं। गायत्री मेरे शीर्ष की, सावित्री देवता ललाट की, वेदमाता नेत्रों की और विश्वामित्र ऋषि कानों की रक्षा करें। गायत्री नासिका की, सावित्री ओष्ठों की, सरस्वती जिह्वा की और शान्तिदा कण्ठ की रक्षा करें। त्रिपदा गायत्री बुद्धि-मेधा-हृदय की, परा शक्ति प्राण की और भुवनेश्वरी अपान की रक्षा करें। गायत्री मन्त्र के प्रत्येक पद सभी दिशाओं से मेरी रक्षा करें। विद्यार्थी को विद्या, धनार्थी को धन, पुत्रार्थी को पुत्र और मोक्षार्थी को मोक्ष प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish a divine protective armor around the devotee
Protects all body parts and vital energies (pranas)
Students gain knowledge and academic success
Traditionally recited for removal of all accumulated sins
Invokes ultimate liberation when recited at three sandhyas daily
description: The Gayatri Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as श्री गायत्री कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of Goddess Gayatri. Attributed to Devi Bhagavatam, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to seeker of wealth attain prosperity and destroy all accumulated sins. It is also said to protect all body parts and vital energies (pranas). This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Gayatri Jayanti to invoke divine grace. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gayatri Kavacham in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Divine protective armor of Gayatri Mata for complete protection.

Gayatri mantra Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur
Gayatri Jayanti Jyeshtha Shukla Ekadashi date
Veda Mata Gayatri five faces ten arms iconography
Brahma Sandhya Madhyahnika Sayam morning evening
Shantikunj Haridwar Yug Rishi Gayatri Pariwar

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Kavacham and what protection does it offer?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Kavacham (kavacha = armour) is a Sanskrit protective hymn invoking Goddess Gayatri — the personified form of the Gayatri Mantra (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat) of Rigveda 3.62.10, addressed to Savitr (the solar deity). She is hailed as Veda-Mata (mother of the Vedas) and Chhandasam-Mata (mother of all Vedic metres). The kavacham systematically requests Gayatri to protect each part of the devotee's body and each direction, invoking her in her three forms (Gayatri-Savitri-Saraswati for morning-noon-evening). It is preserved in the Devi Bhagavatam Purana and is the central protective sadhana of all dvijas (twice-born) who have undergone upanayanam. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam Purana; Gayatri Tantra; Hindupedia Gayatri.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Gayatri and why is she called Veda Mata?
seoFaqs.answer: Goddess Gayatri is the personified form of the Gayatri Mantra, regarded as the supreme mantra of the Vedas and the source from which all Vedic knowledge flows — hence her epithet Veda-Mata (mother of the Vedas). Iconographically she is depicted with five faces (representing the five elements / the five sheaths / the five Vedic mantras) and ten arms (holding all the Vedic implements including the kamandalu, akshamala, ankusha, pasha, padma, conch, discus and gada), seated on a swan or lotus, golden-hued. She is also identified as the consort of Brahma in his Veda-revealing aspect, and is worshipped in three forms during the day: Gayatri at sunrise (red-hued, child Brahma-shakti), Savitri at noon (white-hued, mature Vishnu-shakti), and Saraswati at sunset (dark-hued, elder Shiva-shakti). (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam Purana; Hindupedia Gayatri iconography; Vedic tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Mantra and its meaning?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra is the most celebrated mantra of the Vedas, found at Rigveda 3.62.10 attributed to Sage Vishvamitra: Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah / Tat Savitur Varenyam / Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi / Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat — Om, the earth, the atmosphere, the heavens; that supreme excellent splendour of Savitr (the solar deity), we meditate upon; may he illumine our intellect. The mantra is in the gayatri metre (24 syllables in three lines of eight), giving the goddess her name. It is the central mantra of the Sandhyavandanam thrice-daily prayer, the mantra given at upanayanam (sacred-thread initiation), and is regarded as encapsulating the essence of all Vedas, Upanishads and the Brahma-vidya itself. (Sources: Rigveda 3.62.10; Brihadaranyaka Upanishad; Hindupedia Gayatri Mantra.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Gayatri Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are the three sandhyas (sunrise, noon and sunset — the prescribed times for daily Gayatri recitation), Gayatri Jayanti (Jyeshtha Shukla Ekadashi, June, the day Vishvamitra received the mantra-darshan), Upakarma / Avani Avittam (Shravana Purnima, when the sacred thread is renewed), every Ekadashi, the entire Shravana month, on every solar transit (Sankranti), and at the upanayanam ceremony of any household member. Daily recitation in brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM, the prime sandhya time) facing east, after performing the morning achamana and pranayama, before commencing the 108-fold Gayatri japa, is the prescribed Smarta sadhana. The kavacham is typically recited once before the japa as a protective enclosure. (Sources: Drikpanchang Gayatri Jayanti; Sandhyavandanam vidhi; Hindupedia Gayatri sadhana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Sandhyavandanam in Gayatri worship?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sandhyavandanam is the prescribed thrice-daily ritual of Gayatri worship for all dvijas (twice-born initiates), performed at the three sandhyas (junctions): sunrise (pratah-sandhya, when Gayatri appears as the rising sun), noon (madhyahnika-sandhya, when Savitri presides as the zenith sun), and sunset (sayam-sandhya, when Saraswati dissolves the day). Each sandhya involves achamana (purification), pranayama (breath control), arghya (offering of water to the sun three times — the central act, said to support the sun's chariot), upasthana (standing prayers), and Gayatri japa (108 or 1008 repetitions). The Gayatri Kavacham is recited as part of the protective preliminary (kavacha-nyasa), invoking the goddess on each part of the body before the japa proper. (Sources: Yajurveda Sandhya bhashya; Smarta sadhana texts; Hindupedia Sandhyavandanam.)

seoFaqs.question: Who can recite the Gayatri Mantra and Gayatri Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally the Gayatri Mantra was restricted to dvijas (Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas) after upanayanam (sacred-thread initiation, typically between ages 7-12), since it is considered the supreme mantra requiring the discipline of Brahmacharya. However, the modern reformer Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya, founder of the Gayatri Pariwar at Shantikunj Haridwar, propagated the view that all human beings — regardless of caste, gender or background — have the right to chant the Gayatri Mantra. Today most acharyas, including Swami Vivekananda, Swami Sivananda, Sri Aurobindo and the leading sampradayas, accept that the Gayatri Mantra and Kavacham may be recited by anyone with sincere devotion and basic ritual purity. The mantra is recited by millions of women and householders globally. (Sources: Gayatri Pariwar tradition; Hindupedia Gayatri inclusivity; Modern Smarta consensus.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gayatri Kavacham promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants Brahma-vidya (the highest non-dual knowledge), purification of the buddhi (intellect — the precise blessing the mantra requests), success in academic and spiritual pursuits, removal of all categories of papa (sin) accumulated across lifetimes, protection from black magic and tantric attacks, healing from mental and emotional disturbances, longevity (since Gayatri-japa is said to add years to life), removal of obstacles in marriage and progeny, success in competitive examinations and judicial matters, recovery of memory and concentration, freedom from the gravest karmic obstructions, and ultimately moksha — for the Gayatri Mantra is regarded as encoding the essence of all Vedas and Upanishads, and its full mastery is equivalent to Self-realisation. (Sources: Devi Bhagavatam phalashruti; Gayatri Tantra; Hindupedia Gayatri upasana.)
